title: ellipeiopsis cherrevensis
side_effects: Ellipeiopsis cherrevensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's alkaloids can lead to more severe issues, including respiratory problems, and even liver damage in some cases. Ingesting the plant's leaves or roots can also trigger allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching. Additionally, using Ellipeiopsis cherrevensis as a treatment for medical conditions without proper guidance can worsen existing health problems or interact with other medications.
